reason: The missing `RuntimeException` and `Error` hints in s2 have low user impact. The existing hint `例外処理` already routes exception-related queries to s2. The section content itself contains these class names in both prose and code, so full-text retrieval is unaffected. A user asking about checked-vs-unchecked exception behavior in `handle()` would land on s2 via `例外処理` regardless. The only gap is queries using the exact PascalCase class name as a search term, which is an uncommon entry point for this architectural concept.

findings_assessment.justification: The existing hint `例外処理` is sufficient to route exception-related queries to s2. The section prose and code sample already contain `RuntimeException` and `Error` verbatim, so content-level retrieval is unaffected. Adding these class names as hints would only marginally improve routing for queries using bare Java class names as entry points — an unlikely query pattern for an architectural overview topic. Phase E left this unchanged, likely treating it as below the fix threshold given the minor severity rating.
